metadata.title: UPSC Mains GS Paper 3 — Internal Security Comprehensive Syllabus
metadata.purpose: Standalone Internal Security file for MentorsDaily UPSC portal — AI module generation
metadata.note: Internal Security is dynamic — current affairs integration essential. Update schemes and incidents annually.

book_4.title: Ministry of Home Affairs Annual Report
book_4.usage: Latest data on insurgency, security forces, schemes

book_5.title: IDSA Reports
book_5.publisher: Institute for Defence Studies and Analyses

modules.module_name: Linkages Between Development and Extremism — Left Wing Extremism

topics.topic_name: Left Wing Extremism — Causes, Geography, and Ideology
topics.primary_source: MHA Annual Report; Ashok Kumar Ch 4 — LWE; IDSA LWE reports
key_concepts: LWE defined — Naxalite/Maoist insurgency inspired by Charu Mazumdar (1967 Naxalbari uprising)
Ideological roots — Marxism-Leninism-Maoism, peasant revolution theory
CPI (Maoist) — formed 2004 merger of PWG and MCC — primary Maoist outfit
Red Corridor — 90 districts in 10 states — Jharkhand, Chhattisgarh, Odisha, Telangana, AP core
Peak 2009-2010 — 2000+ incidents, declined to 400-500 range by 2023
Root causes: land alienation, forest rights denial, tribal displacement, development deficit
Development deprivation link — aspirational districts overlap with LWE districts
Salwa Judum — state-sponsored counter-militia — SC declared unconstitutional 2011
People's Liberation Guerrilla Army (PLGA) — Maoist armed wing
Jan Adalats — parallel governance structures in Maoist territory
Extortion from contractors, forest produce — economic sustenance
Recruitment from marginalised communities — tribals, landless labourers
Urban Maoist network — Bhima Koregaon case — alleged urban support structure
LWE states: Jharkhand, Chhattisgarh, Odisha, Maharashtra, Telangana, AP, Bihar, MP, Kerala, West Bengal
SAMADHAN strategy 2017 — Smart leadership, Aggressive strategy, Motivation, Action plan, Dashboard, Human terrain mapping, Action plans for each theatre, No access for LWE to financing
related_laws_acts: UAPA 1967 amended 2019 — Unlawful Activities Prevention Act — designating organisations and individuals
CSPSA — Chhattisgarh Special Public Security Act — state-level anti-Naxal law
Forest Rights Act 2006 — addressing tribal grievance underlying LWE
Scheduled Tribes and Other Traditional Forest Dwellers Act — overlaps with LWE area
current_affairs_peg: CRPF anti-LWE operations — Bastar region — declining Maoist violence 2022-2024
Encounter killings in Chhattisgarh — transparency and accountability debate
Surrender and rehabilitation policy — results in key states
topics.pyq_frequency: Very High — asked in GS3 every 2 years

topics.primary_source: MHA Annual Report; SAMADHAN policy document; Ashok Kumar Ch 5
key_concepts: SAMADHAN doctrine — 8-point strategy for LWE eradication
Security grid — CRPF, CoBRA battalions, state police coordination
CoBRA (Commando Battalion for Resolute Action) — CRPF specialised jungle warfare unit
Bastariya Battalion — local tribal recruitment into CRPF for LWE areas
Road connectivity — PMGSY roads in LWE districts — breaking isolation
Mobile connectivity — towers in LWE-affected villages
PVTG mission — addressing tribal grievance in most vulnerable areas
Surrender and rehabilitation policy — state-specific schemes
Intelligence grid — IB, state intelligence, technical surveillance integration
Riverine policing — water body surveillance in Maoist movement areas
LWE security related expenditure scheme — central assistance to states
Special Infrastructure Scheme — police stations, outposts in LWE areas
Civic action programmes — building trust between security forces and tribals
Media management in LWE areas — counter-narrative to Maoist propaganda
Post-surrender rehabilitation — education, skill training, employment
related_laws_acts: UAPA — legal framework for prosecution of LWE cadre
AFSPA not applicable in LWE areas — key distinction from Northeast insurgency
current_affairs_peg: 2024 operations — multiple encounters, top Maoist leadership neutralised
District-wise reduction in LWE violence — MHA quarterly data

sample_questions: Examine the linkage between development deficit and Left Wing Extremism in India. (10 marks)
Critically evaluate the SAMADHAN strategy for countering LWE. Has it been effective? (12.5 marks)

modules.module_name: Terrorism — Types, Threats and Counter-Terrorism Framework

topics.topic_name: Terrorism — Types, Causes and Ideological Basis
topics.primary_source: 2nd ARC Report 8; Ashok Kumar Ch 2 — Terrorism; IDSA terrorism reports
key_concepts: Terrorism defined — use of violence to instil fear for political, ideological, religious ends
Types: Ethno-nationalist, religious, ideological (LWE), lone-wolf, state-sponsored
Cross-border terrorism — Pakistan-based outfits — LeT, JeM, HM operating in J&K
Islamic radicalisation — SIMI (banned 2001), ISIS-inspired lone wolves, PFI
Right-wing extremism — cow vigilantism, anti-minority violence — emerging concern
Domestic vs transnational terrorism distinction
Funding sources — hawala, narco-terrorism, kidnapping for ransom, diaspora
Recruitment — online radicalisation, social media, local grievances
FATF — Financial Action Task Force — grey-listing impact on Pakistan
Counter-terrorism architecture — NIA, NSG, NTRO, IB, RAW coordination
NIA Act 2008 — National Investigation Agency — post 26/11 creation
UAPA 1967 amended 2012, 2019 — bail provisions, individual designation
NSG — National Security Guard — anti-hijacking, counter-terror rapid response
26/11 Mumbai attacks — intelligence failure analysis, lessons for architecture
Pulwama 2019 and Balakot air strike — cross-border terrorism response doctrine
related_laws_acts: UAPA 2019 amendment — individual designation as terrorist without trial
NIA Act 2008 — federal terrorism investigation agency
NDPS Act — narco-terrorism link
Prevention of Money Laundering Act (PMLA) — terror financing
current_affairs_peg: Pahalgam attack 2025 — cross-border terrorism escalation
FATF review of terror financing globally
Online radicalisation — NIA chargesheeting of ISIS-inspired youth

sample_questions: Examine the nature and sources of funding for terrorist organisations in India. (10 marks)
Discuss the challenges in countering cross-border terrorism. What institutional mechanisms has India developed? (12.5 marks)

modules.module_name: North-East India — Insurgency and Peace Process

topics.topic_name: North-East Insurgency — Causes, Groups and Peace Accords
topics.primary_source: Ashok Kumar Ch 6 — Northeast; MHA Annual Report NE section
key_concepts: Historical roots — partition, demographic change, neglect of distinct cultures
ULFA (United Liberation Front of Asom) — Assam independence demand, 1979
NSCN-IM vs NSCN-K — Naga insurgency split — Framework Agreement 2015 with IM
Bodo insurgency — NDFB factions — Bodoland demand — peace accord 2020
Manipur insurgency — Meitei-Kuki-Naga ethnic conflict, PREPAK, UNLF
Mizo peace accord 1986 — Laldenga — model for peace negotiation
Tripura — NLFT, ATTF — largely resolved through development
Meghalaya — Garo and Khasi militant groups — lower intensity
AFSPA 1958 — Armed Forces Special Powers Act — controversial in NE
Irom Sharmila — 16-year fast against AFSPA — symbol of civil protest
Supreme Court Extrajudicial Execution Probe — fake encounter accountability
Look East/Act East policy — economic integration as security dividend
Inner Line Permit (ILP) — demographic protection mechanism
6th Schedule — Autonomous District Councils in Assam, Meghalaya, Tripura, Mizoram
Article 371 special provisions — various Northeast states protections
related_laws_acts: AFSPA 1958 — disturbed area declaration, search and arrest powers for military
Article 371 (A-J) — special provisions for NE states
6th Schedule — ADC powers over tribal areas
current_affairs_peg: Manipur ethnic violence 2023-24 — Meitei-Kuki conflict, AFSPA withdrawal
Naga peace talks — Framework Agreement implementation stalled
Assam-Meghalaya border dispute resolution process

sample_questions: Examine the root causes of insurgency in North-East India. How has India addressed them? (12.5 marks)
Critically evaluate the Armed Forces Special Powers Act. Should it be repealed? (10 marks)

topics.topic_name: J&K — Cross-Border Terrorism, Radicalisation and Security Grid
topics.primary_source: MHA Annual Report; 2nd ARC Report 8; Ashok Kumar Ch 7
key_concepts: Article 370 abrogation August 2019 — J&K to two UTs: J&K and Ladakh
Cross-border terrorism — ISI-backed groups: LeT, JeM, Hizbul Mujahideen
LOC infiltration — barrier fencing, floodlights, integrated surveillance system
Proxy war doctrine — Pakistan-sponsored terrorism without direct warfare
Home-grown militancy — local Kashmiri joining militant ranks
Online radicalisation — social media recruitment by Pakistan-based handlers
Stone-pelting — hybrid warfare combining mass protest and terrorism cover
Hybrid militants — ordinary citizens moonlighting as overground workers
J&K Police — central role, Surrendered Militant Policy
Army's Rashtriya Rifles — counter-insurgency grid in J&K
CASO — Cordon and Search Operations — standard counter-insurgency tool
Pulwama 2019 CRPF convoy attack — 40 soldiers killed — JeM responsibility
Balakot strike 2019 — first cross-border air strike post-independence
Targeted killings 2021-22 — Hindu and Kashmiri Pandit migrants
Pahalgam terrorist attack April 2025 — 26 tourists killed — escalation
related_laws_acts: J&K Public Safety Act 1978 — preventive detention up to 2 years
UAPA — primary law for J&K terrorism cases
AFSPA applicable in J&K — Disturbed Areas Act
current_affairs_peg: Pahalgam attack April 2025 and India's response — Operation Sindoor May 2025
Restoration of statehood to J&K — SC direction, electoral process
Return of Kashmiri Pandits — PM package implementation

sample_questions: Analyse the security challenges in Jammu and Kashmir after the abrogation of Article 370. (12.5 marks)
What is proxy war? How has Pakistan used it against India? (10 marks)

modules.module_name: Organised Crime, Narco-Terrorism and Money Laundering

topics.topic_name: Organised Crime, Drug Trafficking and Terror Financing
topics.primary_source: Ashok Kumar Ch 8; UNODC reports; NIA annual report
key_concepts: Organised crime defined — structured criminal enterprise for profit
D-Company (Dawood Ibrahim) — Mumbai underworld, ISI nexus
Hawala networks — informal money transfer for terror financing
Narco-terrorism — drug profits funding terrorist groups
Golden Crescent (Afghanistan-Pakistan-Iran) — heroin supply to India
Golden Triangle (Myanmar-Laos-Thailand) — methamphetamine supply
Punjab drug problem — cross-border smuggling via Pakistan
NDPS Act 1985 — Narcotic Drugs and Psychotropic Substances Act
NCB — Narcotics Control Bureau — apex drug law enforcement
FATF grey-listing of Pakistan — global financing framework
Cryptocurrency for terror financing — dark web, VPN use
Money laundering — placement, layering, integration stages
PMLA 2002 — Prevention of Money Laundering Act — ED jurisdiction
UNODC — UN Office on Drugs and Crime — international cooperation
India's extradition treaties — bringing fugitives back
related_laws_acts: NDPS Act 1985 — mandatory minimum sentences for drug offences
PMLA 2002 — attachment, prosecution of money laundering
UAPA — terror financing provisions
Foreign Exchange Management Act (FEMA) — hawala monitoring
current_affairs_peg: ED operations against hawala networks linked to terrorism
Drug seizures at India-Pakistan border — record hauls
Cryptocurrency terror financing — NIA investigations

sample_questions: Explain the linkage between drug trafficking and terrorism with reference to India. (10 marks)
What is hawala? How does it facilitate terror financing and what measures exist to counter it? (12.5 marks)

topics.primary_source: Ashok Kumar Ch 10 — Cyber Security; NCSC reports; CERT-In advisories
key_concepts: Cyber security dimensions — confidentiality, integrity, availability (CIA triad)
Cyber threats: hacking, phishing, malware, ransomware, DDoS, APTs
Critical Information Infrastructure (CII) — power, banking, telecom, defence
NCIIPC — National Critical Information Infrastructure Protection Centre
CERT-In — Computer Emergency Response Team India — nodal agency
IT Act 2000 — amended 2008 — cyber crime provisions (Sections 66, 66A struck down)
Cyber warfare — state-sponsored attacks on infrastructure (China, Pakistan on India)
Dark web — anonymous networks for terrorism, drugs, child pornography
Social media manipulation — disinformation, fake news in security context
Pegasus spyware — surveillance of activists and journalists — civil liberties debate
Deepfakes — AI-generated videos for disinformation
Internet shutdown — use for security vs economic and rights cost
5G security concerns — Chinese equipment (Huawei/ZTE) in telecom infrastructure
National Cyber Security Policy 2013 — updated framework needed
India-specific: RBI cyber security framework for banks, NPCI digital security
related_laws_acts: IT Act 2000 and 2008 amendment — cyber crime provisions
Digital Personal Data Protection Act 2023 — data sovereignty
IT (Amendment) Rules 2021 — social media intermediary obligations
NCIIPC Guidelines — CII protection framework
current_affairs_peg: AIIMS ransomware attack 2022 — critical health infrastructure vulnerability
Chinese hacking attempts on Indian power grid — Ladakh standoff context
New cyber security rules 2023 — 6-hour reporting mandate to CERT-In
topics.pyq_frequency: Very High — dynamic topic asked frequently

sample_questions: What are the major cyber security challenges facing India? Examine the institutional architecture to address them. (12.5 marks)
Critically examine the role of social media in internal security threats. (10 marks)

topics.topic_name: Land Border Management — India's Multiple Frontiers
topics.primary_source: Ashok Kumar Ch 11 — Border Management; MHA Annual Report
key_concepts: India's land borders — 15,106 km with 7 countries
Indo-Pakistan border — 3,323 km — LOC + International Boundary — BSF primary
Indo-China border — 3,488 km — LAC — ITBP primary, Army support
Indo-Bangladesh border — 4,096 km — fencing challenge, migration, cattle smuggling
Indo-Nepal border — 1,751 km — open border — people-to-people ties
Indo-Bhutan border — 699 km — friendly, security cooperation
Indo-Myanmar border — 1,643 km — FCMA (Free Movement Regime) — now restricted
Border fencing — IB with Pakistan largely fenced; Bangladesh ongoing
Border Area Development Programme (BADP) — development in border villages
Vibrant Villages Programme — 663 border villages — countering China in Arunachal, Ladakh
Integrated Check Posts (ICPs) — single-window border crossing
CIBMS — Comprehensive Integrated Border Management System — smart surveillance
Tunnel detection technology — anti-infiltration at Pakistan border
Sealing borders vs open borders — trade-off in Nepal and Bangladesh context
Doklam 2017 and Galwan 2020 — LAC standoff management lessons
related_laws_acts: Foreigners Act 1946 — deportation of illegal migrants
Citizenship Amendment Act 2019 — religion-based citizenship, border migration nexus
BSF Act 1968 — jurisdiction expanded to 50 km in 2021
current_affairs_peg: Galwan Valley standoff 2020 — infrastructure race on LAC
Bangladesh border fence completion — illegal migration control
Myanmar junta instability — refugee influx through Mizoram and Manipur

topics.primary_source: Ashok Kumar Ch 12 — Maritime Security; MHA Coastal Security Scheme
key_concepts: India's coastline — 7,516 km — 13 coastal states and UTs
26/11 sea route — weakness that led to complete coastal security overhaul
Multi-tier coastal security: Coast Guard, Navy, Marine Police, Customs
NCSMCS — National Committee for Strengthening Maritime and Coastal Security
Coastal Security Scheme — police stations, boats, infrastructure
SOP for coastal security — information sharing matrix
Vessel Monitoring System — AIS (Automatic Identification System) mandatory
Fishing community — eyes and ears — coastal community policing
ISAC — Information Sharing and Analysis Centre for maritime
IOR — Indian Ocean Region — India's strategic maritime interests
Piracy — Gulf of Aden, Strait of Malacca — Indian Navy anti-piracy
Blue economy vs security — reconciling fishing, shipping, security
Island security — A&N Islands, Lakshadweep — strategic importance
Maritime terrorism — sea-based infiltration, weapons smuggling
SAGAR doctrine — Security and Growth for All in the Region
related_laws_acts: Suppression of Unlawful Acts Against Safety of Maritime Navigation and Fixed Platforms Act 2002
Maritime Zones Act 1976 — EEZ, continental shelf jurisdiction
Coast Guard Act 1978 — powers and jurisdiction
current_affairs_peg: Red Sea attacks by Houthis — impact on India's trade and naval response
Indian Navy anti-piracy operations 2024 — INS deployment in Indian Ocean
Sri Lanka fishing disputes — maritime boundary management

sample_questions: Critically evaluate India's coastal security architecture after the 26/11 attacks. (12.5 marks)
Examine the challenges in managing India's land borders. Which border is most difficult to manage and why? (10 marks)

modules.module_name: Security Forces — Structure, Role and Police Reforms

topics.topic_name: Central Armed Police Forces and Intelligence Agencies
topics.primary_source: MHA Annual Report; Ashok Kumar Ch 13-14 — Security Forces
key_concepts: CAPF under MHA — CRPF, BSF, CISF, ITBP, SSB, NSG, AR (Assam Rifles)
CRPF — Central Reserve Police Force — largest, anti-insurgency (LWE + J&K)
BSF — Border Security Force — India-Pakistan and India-Bangladesh borders
CISF — Central Industrial Security Force — airports, PSUs, nuclear installations
ITBP — Indo-Tibetan Border Police — India-China LAC deployment
SSB — Sashastra Seema Bal — India-Nepal and India-Bhutan borders
NSG — National Security Guard — anti-hijacking, counter-terror, VVIP protection
Assam Rifles — oldest paramilitary — Northeast deployment under Army operational control
IB — Intelligence Bureau — domestic intelligence, counter-espionage
RAW — Research and Analysis Wing — foreign intelligence, India's external spy agency
NIA — National Investigation Agency — federal terror investigation
NTRO — National Technical Research Organisation — technical intelligence
DIA — Defence Intelligence Agency — military intelligence coordination
Dual control problem — Assam Rifles under MHA but Army command
CAPF modernisation — weapons, bulletproof vests, UAVs, communication
related_laws_acts: NIA Act 2008 — establishment and powers of NIA
CRPF Act 1949 — governing CRPF
BSF Act 1968 — border security powers
current_affairs_peg: CDS position — Chief of Defence Staff — unified military command
Agnipath scheme — armed forces recruitment changes and security implications
CAPF vacancy crisis — thousands of posts unfilled

topics.topic_name: Police Reforms — Prakash Singh Case, Model Police Act
topics.primary_source: 2nd ARC Report 9; Prakash Singh judgment 2006; BPRD Model Police Act
key_concepts: Prakash Singh v Union of India 2006 — landmark SC direction for police reforms
7 directives: Security of tenure, police establishment board, police complaints authority, NSC, separation of investigation from law & order, SGPS
Model Police Act 2006 — draft replacing Police Act 1861 — few states adopted
Police Act 1861 — colonial legacy — designed for control not service
State Police — subject under Entry 2 List II — Centre-State tension
Police-public relations — NPA data, poor trust especially among SC/ST/minorities
Custodial deaths — NHRC data — accountability gaps
Third degree torture — prevalent, legally prohibited
Encounter killings debate — fake encounters vs genuine operations
Police modernisation scheme — weapons, vehicles, communication
Smart policing — predictive policing, data analytics, CCTVs
Community policing — Janamaithri (Kerala), Mitra Dala (Odisha) models
Women in police — 10% target, currently 12.5% — representation matters for reporting
Criminal justice reforms — linked to police reform — fast courts, forensics
Forensic Science — forensic labs modernisation, digital forensics capacity
related_laws_acts: Police Act 1861 — colonial law still governing most state police forces
CrPC 1973 — procedural law governing police investigation and arrest
BNSS 2023 — Bharatiya Nagarik Suraksha Sanhita — replaces CrPC
current_affairs_peg: SC's repeated push for Prakash Singh compliance — most states defiant
New criminal laws (BNS, BNSS, BSA) — 2023 — police powers redefined
Delhi Police encounters — accountability and oversight debate

sample_questions: Examine the key recommendations of the Prakash Singh judgment on police reforms. Why have they not been implemented? (12.5 marks)
Discuss the role and functions of India's intelligence agencies in internal security. (10 marks)

modules.module_name: Role of External State and Non-State Actors in Insurgency

topics.topic_name: External Support to Insurgency — Pakistan, China, Non-State Actors
topics.primary_source: IDSA reports; Ashok Kumar Ch 15-16 — External threats
key_concepts: ISI (Inter-Services Intelligence) — Pakistan's agency supporting terrorism in India
ISI-terrorist nexus — LeT, JeM — training, funding, logistics
Pakistan's strategy — bleed India by a thousand cuts — proxy war doctrine
China's support to Northeast insurgencies — historical (NSCN-IM weapons, ULFA links)
China's current strategy — grey zone warfare, disinformation, economic leverage
CPEC and strategic encirclement concern — Gwadar, Sri Lanka, Bangladesh
Bangladesh — Hasina government cooperation vs post-coup Yunus government uncertainty
Nepal — Chinese influence, Madhesi question, open border exploitation
Sri Lanka — Tamil diaspora funding historical — LTTE external support model
ISIS global network — recruitment from India — Kerala, Hyderabad cases
Diaspora funding — Khalistani movement in Canada, UK, Australia
Khalistan — resurgence of Sikh separatism among diaspora post-2020
Non-state actors — international jihadi networks, Al-Qaeda in Indian Subcontinent
Grey zone warfare — short of armed conflict but destabilising
India's response — diplomatic pressure, counter-intelligence, surgical strikes
related_laws_acts: UAPA — designation of foreign terrorist organisations
FCRA — Foreign Contribution (Regulation) Act — prevent foreign funding of NGOs for insurgency
Extradition treaties and INTERPOL red notices
current_affairs_peg: Hardeep Singh Nijjar killing (Canada) — Khalistan and India-Canada diplomatic crisis
Pannun assassination plot — US warning to India — cross-border operations legality
Operation Sindoor May 2025 — India's response to Pahalgam attack

sample_questions: Examine Pakistan's role in sponsoring terrorism in India. What diplomatic and security tools has India used to counter it? (12.5 marks)
Discuss the role of the diaspora in funding insurgency movements in India. (10 marks)

daily_routine.reading: 1.5 hours — Ashok Kumar primary + MHA Annual Report for data
daily_routine.current_affairs_integration: 30 minutes — link daily news to Internal Security modules

full_study_plan.current_affairs_note: Internal Security questions are increasingly current-affairs based. Read The Hindu/Indian Express security correspondents daily.

output_format.daily_target: topic_id + key acts to memorise + current affairs link
output_format.news_link_of_day: recent event connecting to today's topic
